Mouse Olfactory Receptors + Human and Rat Orthologs

Olfactory Receptors (ORs) are the largest family of G-protein coupled receptors (GPCRs) that bind to molecular ligands that activate the associated G-protein, leading to downstream activation of cellular processes.

Database published in the following manuscript: Poll BG, Chen L, Chou CL, Raghuram V, Knepper MA. Landscape of GPCR expression along the mouse nephron. Am J Physiol Renal Physiol. 2021 Jul 1;321(1):F50-F68. doi: 10.1152/ajprenal.00077.2021. Epub 2021 May 24. PMID: 34029142

Published by the Epithelial Systems Biology Laboratory (Mark Knepper, Chief) at the National Heart, Lung and Blood Institute as part of the Kidney Systems Biology Project.

The database includes 1109 genes. Ensembl IDs link to their respective entries. Human gene symbols link to their corresponding GTEX entry. Click to download processed data as spreadsheet. To view the list of all other mouse GPCRs, click here

To search, use browser's Find command (Ctrl-F). Scroll horizontally to see human and rat orthologs.
